What's your coffee order when you're at a café in Mexico — do you stick to familiar drinks or use ordering as a way to explore local specialties?
When you're buying clothing, how much does brand transparency (who made it, where, under what conditions) factor into your decision? Is there a price point where you stop caring as much?
Between online shopping and in-person browsing, what's your split? Do you research heavily online before buying in stores, or do you prefer one channel entirely?

What does your ideal 5-year roadmap look like? Are there specific milestones (financial, career, location, relationship) you actively plan toward, or do you prefer to stay flexible and let opportunities emerge?
When you meet new people in a city (at volleyball, coworking, etc.), how do you typically decide whether to invest in building a deeper friendship versus keeping it as a casual acquaintance?

What's your ideal temperature range when choosing a city? Are you someone who tolerates any heat as long as there's A/C, or do you have a hard upper limit where the heat starts affecting your mood or productivity?

When you're going out for drinks in Mexico — say Friday after volleyball — what's your default? Do you stick to beer, cocktails, or does it depend on the venue? Any go-to Mexican beers or cocktails you always order?

How's your Spanish coming along? Do you feel comfortable handling daily life (ordering food, Negotiating prices, doctor visits) in Spanish, or are there specific situations where you still need English or a translator?

What podcasts do you regularly listen to — if any? Are you into tech/AI podcasts, true crime, storytelling, or something else entirely? Do you mainly listen during commutes, workouts, or while doing chores?

Beyond volleyball, what other hobbies or creative outlets do you enjoy? Things like gaming, photography, music, woodworking, coding side projects — anything you do to unwind that isn't fitness or media consumption?

When you're learning Spanish, what specific scenarios feel most uncomfortable or where do you still get stuck? Is it more around formal vs. casual speech, understanding fast conversations, or expressing nuanced emotions/ideas?

When you're buying something — say a new gadget, clothes, or even food — how often do you actively choose products based on their environmental impact or company ethics? Is it a dealbreaker, a nice-to-have, or something you think about but don't let it override other factors like price or convenience?
What's your current fitness philosophy — are you mostly maintaining, trying to build specific things, or just showing up to feel good? Do you track metrics or go more by how you feel?
Do you have any regular meditation, journaling, or mindfulness practices? Or any spiritual/philosophical frameworks you lean on for perspective?

When things feel off — whether it's work stress, relationship friction, or just general burnout — what's your default way of processing it? Do you talk it through with people, work through it solo, seek professional help, or something else?
